description:
You are a mobile build-pipeline engineer.  
Your CI environment does **not** allow root access, so you must rely on user-space files only.

1. Create the following directory tree (create parent directories if they do not already exist):

   • /home/user/pipeline/config  
   • /home/user/pipeline/scripts  
   • /home/user/pipeline/logs  

2. Inside /home/user/pipeline/config write a shell snippet called **build_env.sh** whose entire, exact contents are:

   export TZ='Europe/Berlin'
   export LC_ALL='en_US.UTF-8'

   (The file must contain **only** those two lines, in that order, followed by a trailing newline.)

3. Create an executable script **/home/user/pipeline/scripts/verify_locale.sh** that:

   • sources /home/user/pipeline/config/build_env.sh  
   • prints the two environment variables in the exact format shown below and writes the output to /home/user/pipeline/logs/locale_check.log, overwriting any previous content of that log.  
   • you may use any interpreter, but the first line must be a proper she-bang (e.g. #!/usr/bin/env bash).

   The log file must end up containing **precisely** the following two lines, nothing more, nothing less, in this exact order and each terminated by a single newline character:

   Timezone: Europe/Berlin
   Locale: en_US.UTF-8

4. After running the script you should be able to open /home/user/pipeline/logs/locale_check.log and see those two lines exactly.

IMPORTANT FORMAT NOTES FOR AUTOMATED TESTING  
• Path names and file names are case-sensitive.  
• build_env.sh must contain exactly 2 lines; the log must contain exactly 2 lines.  
• Both lines in both files must end with a newline (the usual “\n”).  
• Do not add any extra spaces or blank lines.  
• verify_locale.sh itself is not inspected for content, only for execution permission and the fact that it produces the required log file.
truth:
Expected final file system state (all paths absolute):

1. /home/user/pipeline/config/build_env.sh  
   Content (including the trailing newline after the second line):
   export TZ='Europe/Berlin'
   export LC_ALL='en_US.UTF-8'

2. /home/user/pipeline/scripts/verify_locale.sh  
   • File exists and has user-executable bit set (chmod u+x).  
   • May contain any valid shell script; automated grading does NOT parse the script.

3. /home/user/pipeline/logs/locale_check.log  
   Content (including trailing newline after second line):
   Timezone: Europe/Berlin
   Locale: en_US.UTF-8
name: task_000000_2ed5bfd0
